summary: Bridged browser-local Gate Radar notes into the Path Map so matching gate nodes and the route board show private operator memory without changing the snapshot schema.

queries: 2026 dashboard UX annotations notes timeline blockers path visualization best practices
timeline visualizations UX annotations milestones blockers dashboard best practices
operations dashboard comments notes triage blockers UX best practices
pattern_notes: Dashboard annotations should sit close to the decision point so operators do not need to recall context from another view.
Complex dashboards stay usable when they prioritize the next action, use consistent visual hierarchy, and keep scan paths short.
Route and timeline views benefit from progressive disclosure: badges and previews show what matters now, while deeper logs remain available on demand.

ui_contract: Reuse Gate Radar signal ids in Path Map gate nodes so note ids match across views.
Render a note badge and compact preview on matching Path Map gate nodes.
Add a local-only Path Notes card to the Path Map bottom grid.
Add a notes stat to the Path Map stats row.
Keep notes browser-local and avoid mutating web/data/snapshot.json.

red_route.evidence: A seeded Gate Radar note was not visible in Path Map before implementation: no path note panel, preview, noted node, or proof text.

green_route.evidence: A seeded note rendered in Path Map as a Path Notes panel, gate-node preview, note badge, and noted node, with no console errors, no failed requests, and no horizontal overflow.

responsive.evidence: All tested viewports showed the seeded note, Path Notes panel, and noted gate node with no bad rectangles, no horizontal overflow, no console errors, and no failed requests.
